_note: This file is a design-time snapshot and may be out of date. For current step state, run: iw item-status <ID> --json. The DB is the authoritative source of truth (CR-00023).
title: Downgrade LiveDbConnectionRefusedError to DEBUG/WARNING in test context

steps.description: Update dashboard/app.py:142-150 to handle LiveDbConnectionRefusedError separately. In test context (IW_CORE_TEST_CONTEXT=true), log it at DEBUG with a single-line message and no traceback. Outside test context, log at WARNING (no traceback). Keep logger.exception(...) ERROR for any other exception class. Preserve existing behaviour: app.state.alembic_guard_status = None on any failure.

Review S01: scope is exactly the startup probe; the new branch matches LiveDbConnectionRefusedError specifically (not its parent RuntimeError); log levels match the design; no traceback emitted on the refusal path; behaviour of app.state.alembic_guard_status is unchanged; no other exception class accidentally swallowed at a lower level.

Add tests/dashboard/test_live_db_guard_log_level.py with: (1) reproduction test that asserts NO ERROR-level log records mentioning LiveDbConnectionRefused are emitted by dashboard.app during create_app() under IW_CORE_TEST_CONTEXT=true; (2) a positive assertion that app.state.alembic_guard_status is None after refusal; (3) a regression test that monkey-patches check_db_at_head to raise RuntimeError('synthetic') and asserts the existing ERROR + traceback path STILL fires for non-refused exceptions. Use semantic assertions on specific log levels and message substrings, not shape checks.

Review S03: tests fail on pre-fix code (verify by reading the assertions against the current main behaviour), pass on post-fix code, assert specific log levels (>=logging.ERROR) and specific exception class names, do not depend on logger propagation defaults, do not swallow real failures.

Global review across S01 + S03: dashboard/app.py change integrates with the existing guard module without changing the guard's public API; test file lives in tests/dashboard/ and uses the project's caplog + monkeypatch conventions; full unit + integration suites still pass; no regression in alembic guard banner or middleware behaviour.
Self-assessment of the just-completed item via the iw-item-analyze skill
